preserveUserBrands: assets/brands/* except assets/brands/example/ (example is updatable, user brands are not)

featureFlags._doc: Runtime feature flags read at Electron startup. Do NOT rely on these for security-critical defaults — a stale version.json on an offline client stays at the shipped values. Security-critical changes must land as code pushes with a version bump.

notes: v1.19.1: Pre-release Gitar review pass on the bulk-upload PR caught three findings, all fixed before tag: (1) productSlug regex `/^[A-Za-z0-9._-]{1,200}$/` accepted `..` because `.` is in the char class — defense-in-depth check now layers explicit `.`/`..`/all-dots rejection on top via isValidProductSlug helper; (2) 8-char SHA prefix (32 bits) hit 1% birthday-collision threshold at ~9,300 files — bumped to 16 hex (64 bits, ~4B threshold) with SHA_PREFIX_LEN/SHA_PREFIX_RE exported so main.js's prefix-read regex stays in lockstep; (3) sanitizeFilename's slice(0, 200) stripped `.jpg` off pathologically long filenames, then hasAllowedExtension rejected with confusing 'bad-extension' — truncation now preserves the extension, with edge-case handling for ext.length >= MAX_BASENAME_LEN. Three new REGRESSION GUARD blocks + 8 new tests (26 total in bulk-upload.test.js). v1.19.1: Bulk asset upload + auto-product-match. Drag-drop or multi-select photos and videos anywhere in the desktop app and Merlin lands them in your active brand's `assets/brands/<brand>/inbox/` folder. Filenames pass through a new Go matcher (asset_match.go: Jaro-Winkler over a normalization pipeline that strips `IMG_`/`PXL_`/`Screenshot_`/date prefixes and trailing timestamps) which scores against each product's slug, title, and Shopify handle from product.md. High-confidence (>= 0.85 with a > 0.10 gap to runner-up) auto-move into `products/<slug>/references/`. Borderline (0.60-0.85 OR ambiguous twin matches) stay in inbox/ as approval cards. Below 0.60 stay silent — the user clearly knows what they uploaded; don't pester. SHA-256 dedup against everything already on disk so re-dragging the same camera roll twice doesn't create duplicates. Right-click the Bulk upload tile for a folder picker (webkitdirectory). 500 MB/file ceiling, 200 files/drop sanity cap, bounded 6-way parallel hashing so a 50-file drop on a slow disk doesn't thrash IO. v1.19.1: Captions MCP tool. Adds mcp__merlin__captions for burning Hormozi-style word-level captions onto an existing video file using the bundled ffmpeg + whisper-cli + ggml-small.en-q5_1 toolchain that already ships in the installer (.claude/tools/, see release.yml). Pipeline: ffmpeg extracts 16k mono PCM, whisper-cli runs with -ml 1 -oj for word-level timestamps, a libass .ass subtitle file is generated with bold yellow active-word highlighting over a 3-word context bubble (prev + active + next), then ffmpeg burns it in at CRF 18 with audio passthrough. All transcription is local — no upload, no third-party service. Pre-this-tool, when a user asked the in-app agent to add captions, it confabulated ffmpeg isnt installed and offered to write Python; this is the explicit affordance. New: app/captions.js (orchestration + pure ASS generator, 29 unit tests), burn-captions IPC handler in app/main.js (next to transcribe-audio, reuses the same findTool resolution path), captions tool in app/mcp-tools.js (calls into captions.js, maps captions:<step> errors to canonical mcp-error codes with proper next_action hints), Burn captions on existing video subsection in merlin-content SKILL.md routing the agent to the right tool. No new dependencies, no new bundled binaries — just exposes the toolchain that already shipped.
whatsNew: New: Bulk upload tile in the Magic panel + drag-drop anywhere in the app. Drop a folder full of phone photos and Merlin auto-routes the obvious matches into the right product's references/ folder, leaves the borderline ones in inbox/ for one-tap review, and silently skips duplicates by SHA. Right-click the tile for a folder picker.

New: Filename fuzzy-match (Jaro-Winkler) for newly uploaded assets. Strips IMG_/PXL_/Screenshot_/date prefixes and trailing timestamps before scoring against your product slugs, titles, and Shopify handles — so 'IMG_2026_04_28 cloud-zip-up-hoodie_001.jpg' lands in cloud-zip-up-hoodie/references/ on the first try.

Hardened (bulk upload): per-file 500 MB cap, 200-file-per-drop sanity cap, bounded 6-way parallel hashing, allowlisted media extensions only (.png/.jpg/.jpeg/.gif/.webp/.heic/.heif/.mp4/.mov/.webm/.m4v/.avi). 18 helper unit tests + 17 Go matcher tests cover canonical Jaro-Winkler reference values, real-world filename normalization, traversal rejection, and the auto/ambiguous/low-confidence/no-match classifier.

New: mcp__merlin__captions tool — burn Hormozi-style word-level captions onto an existing video using the bundled ffmpeg + whisper-cli + small.en speech model. Local transcription, no upload, no third-party tools.
Hardened: when the in-app agent is asked to add captions to a video, it now routes to a real tool instead of confabulating ffmpeg isnt installed and offering to write Python.

Hardened: 29 new tests cover the ASS subtitle builder (centisecond rounding, libass color escaping, 3-word context bubble, brace escaping in transcript text), input validation (size cap, extension allowlist, absolute path requirement), and toolchain resolution (install-first / workspace-fallback).
